• calculation of moles of HCl used to react with CaO and calculation of moles of NaOH / HCl in titration • calculation of moles of excess HCl (mol NaOH/HCl in titration x 10) • calculation of moles of hydrochloric acid that reacted with calcium oxide (subtraction) • calculation of moles of calcium oxide (division by 2) • calculation of mass of calcium oxide (Multiply by 56.1) • calculation of % purity of calcium oxide (Mass of CaO divided by 5.12 then multiplied by 100) Steps 4-6 must occur after the subtraction step if subtraction has been carried out. (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation 200 ÷ 1000 × 1.00 = 0.200 (mol) 23.70 ÷ 1000 × 0.150 = 3.555 × 10 –3 (mol) 3.555 × 10 –3 × 10 = 3.555 × 10 –2 / 0.03555 (mol) 0.200 – 0.03555 = 0.16445 (mol) 0.16445 ÷ 2 = 0.082225 (mol) 0.082225 × 56.1 = 4.6128 (g) 4.6128 ÷ 5.12 × 100 = 90.094% = 90.1% Allow TE from M1 to M5 Allow TE to M6 if final answer is less than 100% Allow 90%, ignore SF and truncating errors Allow 89.96% obtained from using Mr of 56 Correct answer with no working scores (6) (6) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • Error: calcium (ion) is polarised by the nitrate (ion) and Correction: nitrate (ion) is polarised by calcium (ion) • Error: weakens the bond between the two ions/between calcium and nitrate (ions) and Correction: weakens the (covalent) bond(s) within the nitrate (ion) (1) (1) Allow the calcium (ion) is not polarised (by the nitrate ion) Allow it does not make the bond between the ions weaker Allows weakens the N−O bonds Allow 1 mark for both errors if no credit given for corrections (2) (Total for Question 19 = 14 marks) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • correct dipoles on C and Br atoms • curly arrow from C−Br bond to Br (δ–) or just beyond • hydroxide ion with lone pair on oxygen • curly arrow from lone pair on oxygen to C (δ+) 4 bullet points scores 2 marks 3 or 2 bullet points scores 1 mark 0 or 1 bullet points scores 0 marks Penalise omission / incorrect placement of lon
